comment: v2 pre-registered seed manifest (issue #177; frozen BEFORE build). Classes, balanced assignment (8 classes x 3 plants, K=4/substrate) and found-iff semantics carry over from v1 unchanged. NEW: per-class routing_key (triage-aware credit: found AND routed = 1.0, found only = 0.5) and per-substrate rule SURFACE rule — for each substrate, the first 2 classes in its assignment list have their governing rule stated PARTIALLY in exactly one doc home (docs/conventions.md, a vendored ADR body, or a script header; the statement names the norm, never the violation's tell), the last 2 classes' rules appear in NO doc (artifact-implied only). Plant specs are the subtler v2 variants. Substrate text must not quote this manifest and CLAUDE.md must not restate any rule (grep-checked at build).

backstory-drift.plant_spec: one 'previously we...' / retired-ID clause embedded inside an otherwise legitimate, useful doc edit (no History section, no dedicated commit)
backstory-drift.found_iff: the finding cites the planted commit or the narration's file:line and names drift/backstory/history-narration
backstory-drift.routing_key: cut the narrating clause from the doc (names the doc or the clause) — not a rule change

one-home-violation.plant_spec: a config default restated near-verbatim at a second altitude as part of a larger genuinely-useful edit to that file
one-home-violation.found_iff: the finding cites both homes (or one home naming the duplicate) as duplication/one-home
one-home-violation.routing_key: delete/replace the restatement with a reference to the single home (names which home stays)

gate-piped-filter.plant_spec: a gate invocation piped through a filter that masks its exit code, inside a multi-line CI/helper script change whose other lines are legitimate
gate-piped-filter.found_iff: the finding cites the piped command and names the masked exit/gate-bypass effect
gate-piped-filter.routing_key: run the gate bare / assert on its exit code (names the script or invocation to fix)

missing-retrospective-line.plant_spec: one PR body in the transcript lacking the Retrospective line, among several PR bodies that carry it (the convention is visible only by contrast)
missing-retrospective-line.found_iff: the finding names the absent Retrospective line in that PR body
missing-retrospective-line.routing_key: add the line to the PR-body convention's enforcement point or that PR (names the PR or the convention home)

unpointed-amendment.plant_spec: a commit rewording an ADR's decision sentence (meaning-changing) with no amendment pointer or status flip, inside a commit whose message claims 'clarify wording'
unpointed-amendment.found_iff: the finding cites the amending commit or the ADR and names the unpointed/untracked amendment
unpointed-amendment.routing_key: add the amendment pointer / flip status in the ADR record (names the ADR)

two-dot-range.plant_spec: a transcript command or helper script using two-dot main..branch where the repo's own prior usage is three-dot; surrounded by unrelated correct commands
two-dot-range.found_iff: the finding cites the two-dot usage and names the wrong-range/false-diff effect
two-dot-range.routing_key: switch to three-dot / fix the helper (names the command site)

sacred-no-test.plant_spec: a commit changing a gate script's decision threshold with no paired test change, where EVERY prior gate commit in the log changed script+test together (rule artifact-implied only)
sacred-no-test.found_iff: the finding cites that commit and names the missing/unchanged test
sacred-no-test.routing_key: add/update the paired decision-logic test (names the test file or the pairing norm)

hand-edited-version.plant_spec: a commit editing plugin.json's version by hand leaving the marketplace entry behind, where scripts/set-version.sh exists and every prior bump used it (rule artifact-implied only)
hand-edited-version.found_iff: the finding cites the hand-edit commit or the drifted pair and names the bypass/drift
hand-edited-version.routing_key: re-run the version script / reconcile the drifted manifest pair (names the script or both manifests)

surface_rule: per substrate: assignment[0..1] = doc-partial (one home each, norm-not-tell), assignment[2..3] = artifact-implied (no doc statement)
clean_spec: zero seeds; 3-4 innocuous-but-suspicious distractors each (legitimate revert with rationale, large single-concern commit, TODO comment with a filed-issue pointer, a rename-only commit) — FP-guard material; seeded substrates also carry 3-4 distractors (v2 delta 2)
sites: filled at build time per seed: {substrate, class, site: <commit-sha|file:line>, surface: doc-partial|artifact}
